test_cases.test_case: Successful login with valid credentials
preconditions: User account exists with username 'student1' and known password
User is on the Login page
steps: 2. Enter the correct password into the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Dashboard page; no inline error is shown and user session is established

test_cases.test_case: Login fails with incorrect password — password cleared, username retained
preconditions: User account exists with username 'student1' and known password
User is on the Login page
steps: 2. Enter an incorrect password into the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed indicating login failed; the Password field is cleared; the Username field remains populated with 'student1' for correction

test_cases.test_case: Login fails with non-existing username
preconditions: No account exists for username 'no_such_user'
User is on the Login page
steps: 2. Enter any password into the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed indicating login failed; the Password field is cleared; the Username field remains populated with 'no_such_user'

test_cases.test_case: Login with both fields empty
preconditions: User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed indicating credentials are required; Password field remains empty; Username field remains empty; no redirect occurs

test_cases.test_case: Login with empty password (username provided)
preconditions: User account exists with username 'student1'
User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: An inline error message is displayed indicating password is required; the Password field is empty (cleared); the Username field remains populated with 'student1'; no redirect occurs

test_cases.test_case: Login attempt with very long username and password
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter a very long string (e.g., 500 characters) into the Username field
2. Enter a very long string (e.g., 500 characters) into the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: The application handles the input without crashing; either a validation inline error or authentication failure message is shown; the Password field is cleared; the Username field remains populated (or visibly truncated to UI max length); no unexpected behavior occurs

test_cases.test_case: Access as a guest navigates to unauthenticated browsing
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Click the 'Access as a guest' button
test_cases.expected_result: User is taken to the guest/unauthenticated browsing area (limited-access Dashboard or landing page) without requiring credentials

test_cases.test_case: Lost password? link is present but disabled
preconditions: User is on the Login page
This test site has the 'Lost password?' feature disabled
steps: 1. Attempt to click the 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: 'Lost password?' link does not navigate; link is visibly disabled or inactive and no password recovery flow is started

test_cases.test_case: Cookies notice displays cookie usage information
preconditions: User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: A cookies notice (modal, panel, or message) is displayed containing information about cookie usage and an option to close/dismiss the notice

test_cases.test_case: Attempt login with SQL-injection-like input
preconditions: User is on the Login page
steps: 1. Enter "' OR '1'='1" into the Username field
2. Enter "' OR '1'='1" into the Password field
test_cases.expected_result: Authentication fails and an inline error message is shown; the Password field is cleared; the Username field remains populated; the application does not expose database errors or crash

test_cases.test_case: Login page UI elements presence and required indicators
preconditions: User is on the Login page
test_cases.expected_result: Username and Password fields are visible and marked as required; 'Log in' button is present; 'Lost password?' link is visible (disabled on this site); 'Access as a guest' and 'Cookies notice' buttons are visible

test_cases.test_case: Personalized greeting shows user's display name
preconditions: User is logged in
User has a display name set in profile
test_cases.expected_result: A greeting is visible at the top of the Dashboard and contains the logged-in user's display name (e.g., 'Welcome, <User>')

test_cases.test_case: Timeline displays upcoming activities for default time range (Next 7 days)
preconditions: User is logged in
There are activities with deadlines within the next 7 days across enrolled courses
steps: 3. Ensure the time range dropdown is set to 'Next 7 days'
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline lists the upcoming activities that fall within the next 7 days, each showing name, course, and date/time; items are visible in the current sort order

test_cases.test_case: Changing sort order in Timeline updates item ordering
preconditions: User is logged in
Timeline contains multiple activities with different dates/times
steps: 2. In the Timeline block, open the sort order dropdown
3. Select an alternate sort order (e.g., 'Newest first' or 'Oldest first')
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline items re-order according to the selected sort option (e.g., newest items appear first when 'Newest first' is selected)

test_cases.test_case: Timeline search finds activities by full name
preconditions: User is logged in
There is at least one activity with unique name 'Assignment X' in the current time range
steps: 2. In the Timeline block, enter 'Assignment X' into the search field and submit/search
test_cases.expected_result: Search results show the activity named 'Assignment X'; non-matching items are not shown

test_cases.test_case: Creating a new personal event without required Title shows validation error
preconditions: User is logged in
User is on the Dashboard
User has permission to create personal events
steps: 1. In the Calendar block, click 'New event'
2. In the New Event form leave the Title field empty and fill other required fields if any
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents saving and displays a validation error indicating the Title is required (e.g., 'Please enter a title') and the event is not created

test_cases.test_case: Calendar displays current month/year heading and highlights current date
preconditions: User is logged in
Dashboard Calendar block is visible
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar header shows the current month and year (e.g., 'June 2026') and the cell representing today's date is visually highlighted

test_cases.test_case: Filtering calendar by 'All courses' dropdown shows only events for selected course
preconditions: User is logged in
Calendar contains events from at least two different courses
steps: 2. In the Calendar block, open the 'All courses' dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar updates to display only events that belong to Course A; events from other courses are hidden

test_cases.test_case: Creating a new personal calendar event saves and appears on calendar and timeline if in range
preconditions: User is logged in
User has permission to create personal events
Selected date for event is within timeline time range if verifying timeline
steps: 1. In the Calendar block click 'New event'
2. Fill in valid event details (Title, Date, Time, optional description)
4. Observe the Calendar and (optionally) the Timeline if the event date falls in the selected Timeline range
test_cases.expected_result: Event is created successfully; the new event appears on the calendar cell for the chosen date (showing the title inline) and appears in the Timeline if the date is within the timeline's range

test_cases.test_case: Create event on Feb 29 (leap year) and verify invalid date selection on non-leap year
preconditions: User is logged in
Date picker supports selecting year/month/day
steps: 2. Use date picker to select Feb 29, 2024 (leap year) and save the event
4. Attempt to select Feb 29, 2023 (non-leap year) in date picker
test_cases.expected_result: Event for Feb 29, 2024 is created successfully. For 2023 the date picker prevents selection of Feb 29 or the system shows a validation error if a user attempts to submit that invalid date

test_cases.test_case: Calendar month navigation arrows move calendar to previous/next month
preconditions: User is logged in
Calendar block is visible
steps: 2. Note the month/year in the Calendar header
4. Click the left arrow once (to return)
test_cases.expected_result: After clicking right arrow the calendar header advances by one month; clicking left arrow returns to the original month; calendar cells update to show dates and events for the displayed month

test_cases.test_case: Navigate many months forward/back to verify calendar stability (large range navigation)
preconditions: User is logged in
Calendar navigation is enabled
steps: 2. Click the right arrow repeatedly (e.g., 120 times) to move many months into the future
3. Click the left arrow repeatedly to move back to current month
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar continues to respond without crashing; month/year heading updates correctly for each navigation step; eventually returns to the expected month when navigating back. If the system imposes a navigation limit it shows a graceful message or prevents further navigation without crashing

test_cases.test_case: Dates with events display event names inline in the calendar month view
preconditions: User is logged in
There is at least one event scheduled on a date in the current month
steps: 2. View the current month in the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: Cells for dates that have events show the event name(s) inline within the date cell

test_cases.test_case: 'Full calendar' link opens the dedicated calendar view
preconditions: User is logged in
Dashboard is displayed
steps: 1. Scroll to the bottom of the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: The application navigates to or opens the dedicated calendar view (full calendar page) and the calendar view is displayed

test_cases.test_case: 'Import or export calendars' link opens calendar data management page
preconditions: User is logged in
Dashboard is displayed
steps: 1. Scroll to the bottom of the Calendar block
2. Click the 'Import or export calendars' link
test_cases.expected_result: The calendar import/export or data management page opens (or navigates to the appropriate UI) allowing the user to import or export calendar data

test_cases.test_case: Toggling Edit mode on reveals Reset and Add a block controls
preconditions: User is logged in with page edit permissions
Dashboard is visible
steps: 1. Click the Edit mode toggle to turn Edit mode on
test_cases.expected_result: A 'Reset page to default' button appears at the top right of the Dashboard and a '+ Add a block' button appears below the Dashboard heading

test_cases.test_case: + Add a block opens list of available block types
preconditions: User is logged in with edit permissions
Edit mode is ON
steps: 1. In Edit mode, click the '+ Add a block' button below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: The application opens a page or panel listing all available block types that can be added to the Dashboard

test_cases.test_case: In Edit mode each block shows move icon and three-dot menu with configure/move/delete actions
preconditions: User is logged in with edit permissions
Edit mode is ON
Dashboard contains at least one block (Timeline and Calendar)
steps: 2. Inspect each block on the Dashboard
test_cases.expected_result: Each existing block displays a move icon and a three-dot menu; opening the menu shows options for Configure, Move, and Delete (or equivalent actions)

test_cases.test_case: Reset page to default restores original layout
preconditions: User is logged in with edit permissions
Edit mode is ON
Dashboard layout has been modified (blocks moved/added/removed)
steps: 1. In Edit mode click 'Reset page to default'
test_cases.expected_result: Dashboard reverts to the default page layout; added blocks are removed, moved blocks return to default positions, and a confirmation or success message is shown

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to enter Edit mode without permissions is blocked and edit controls are not shown
preconditions: User is logged in without page edit permissions
steps: 1. Attempt to toggle Edit mode ON from the Dashboard
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents entering Edit mode; Edit-specific controls (Reset, + Add a block, move icons, three-dot menus) are not displayed and an appropriate error or permission message is shown if applicable (e.g., 'You do not have permission to edit this page')

test_cases.test_case: Timeline shows empty state when no items exist within the selected time range
preconditions: User is logged in
No activities exist within a selected timeline range (e.g., 'Next 30 days')
steps: 2. In the Timeline block set the time range to a range with no activities (e.g., 'Next 30 days' when there are none)
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline displays the empty state UI (informational message and/or illustration) indicating there are no upcoming activities within the selected time range

test_cases.test_case: Click course name navigates to course main page
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
My Courses page is accessible
At least one enrolled course exists with a visible course card
steps: 2. Identify a course card and click the course name link
test_cases.expected_result: Browser navigates to the selected course's main page; the URL and page header/course title correspond to the clicked course and course content loads without error

test_cases.test_case: Status filter shows only 'In progress' courses
preconditions: User is logged in
My Courses contains courses with various statuses including 'In progress' and other statuses
test_cases.expected_result: Only course cards whose status is 'In progress' are visible in the grid; courses with other statuses are not shown

test_cases.test_case: Search by partial course name returns matching cards
preconditions: User is logged in
There is at least one course whose name contains a specific recognizable substring (e.g., 'Intro')
steps: 2. Enter the substring (e.g., 'Intro') into the search field
3. Press Enter or click the search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Only course cards whose name includes the substring are displayed; other courses are filtered out

test_cases.test_case: Sort by Name A-Z orders courses alphabetically
preconditions: User is logged in
My Courses contains multiple courses with distinct names
Sort dropdown contains a 'Name A-Z' option
test_cases.expected_result: Course cards are reordered so that their names appear in ascending alphabetical order (A to Z)

test_cases.test_case: Change layout to List view
preconditions: User is logged in
Layout dropdown is visible with options Card, List, Summary
test_cases.expected_result: The page switches to List layout: course presentations change from card grid to vertical list format showing course image, name (link), and category in list rows

test_cases.test_case: Star a course pins it to the top
preconditions: User is logged in
At least one course card is available
The three-dot menu is present on each course card and includes 'Star this course'
steps: 2. On a non-top course card, click the three-dot menu
test_cases.expected_result: The selected course shows a star indicator and is moved/pinned to the top position of the course list/grid (above non-starred courses)

test_cases.test_case: Remove a course from view hides it without unenrolling
preconditions: User is logged in
At least one course card is visible
Three-dot menu includes 'Remove from view'
steps: 2. On a visible course card, click the three-dot menu
test_cases.expected_result: The selected course card is removed/hidden from the My Courses view; user remains enrolled in the course (no unenroll action occurs). The course should be possible to view again via the 'Hidden' status filter or undo if provided

test_cases.test_case: Status filter 'Starred' shows starred/pinned courses
preconditions: User is logged in
At least one course has been starred/pinned
Status dropdown contains 'Starred' option
test_cases.expected_result: Only courses that were starred/pinned are displayed; non-starred courses are not shown

test_cases.test_case: Status filter 'Hidden' shows courses removed from view
preconditions: User is logged in
At least one course has been removed from view (hidden)
Status dropdown contains 'Hidden' option
test_cases.expected_result: Courses previously removed from view are displayed in the list; they remain hidden from other status views until unhidden

test_cases.test_case: Search input with SQL-like special characters does not expose data or crash
preconditions: User is logged in
My Courses page is open
steps: 2. Enter a SQL-like string such as "' OR '1'='1' --" into the search field
3. Press Enter or click the search icon
test_cases.expected_result: The application remains responsive and does not return unintended courses or crash; either no results or only correctly sanitized filtering results are shown and no error stack trace is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Search input exceeding maximum length is rejected or truncated
preconditions: User is logged in
My Courses search field is available
There is a client or server-defined maximum input length (test with a very long string)
steps: 2. Paste a very long string (e.g., 5000 characters) into the search field
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents the excessively long input from causing errors: either the input is truncated client-side, a validation message like 'Search term too long' is shown, or the server returns a handled response (no crash).

test_cases.test_case: Search is case-insensitive (matching different case)
preconditions: User is logged in
There is a course whose name is 'Biology 101' or similar
test_cases.expected_result: Course 'Biology 101' is returned despite case differences, confirming case-insensitive search behavior

test_cases.test_case: Star multiple courses and verify pinned order
preconditions: User is logged in
At least three courses are available
steps: 2. For Course A, open its three-dot menu and select 'Star this course'
3. For Course B, open its menu and select 'Star this course'
4. Observe the order of starred courses at the top
test_cases.expected_result: Both Course A and Course B appear above unstarred courses; order is consistent with defined behavior (e.g., most recently starred first) and both show a star indicator

test_cases.test_case: Hidden course remains hidden after page refresh (persistence)
preconditions: User is logged in
At least one course has been removed from view (hidden)
steps: 1. Open the My Courses page and confirm the course is hidden (not visible in default All view)
3. Re-open the My Courses page or confirm current state
test_cases.expected_result: The previously hidden course remains hidden after refresh; it is still visible when the 'Hidden' status filter is selected, demonstrating persistence of the hidden state

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly toggle layout options to check UI stability
preconditions: User is logged in
Layout dropdown contains Card, List, Summary
steps: 2. Repeatedly change the layout selection between Card, List, and Summary (e.g., 20 rapid changes)
3. Observe the UI behavior and responsiveness
test_cases.expected_result: The UI remains responsive without visual glitches or crashes; each selected layout is rendered correctly after change

test_cases.test_case: Clicking course link while offline shows network error and does not crash
preconditions: User is logged in and on the My Courses page
A course card is present
Test environment can simulate offline/network loss
steps: 2. Click the course name link on a course card
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation fails gracefully with a clear network/error message (e.g., 'You are offline' or 'Unable to load course'); the application does not crash or display an unhandled error

test_cases.test_case: Course heading and navigation tab bar are displayed
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
User is enrolled in the course
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course page for the enrolled course
test_cases.expected_result: Course name is displayed as the page heading and the navigation tab bar is visible below the heading

test_cases.test_case: Expand and collapse individual section using chevron
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Course has at least one collapsed section with activities
steps: 2. Click the chevron icon for a section to expand it
3. Click the chevron icon again to collapse the section
test_cases.expected_result: Section expands to show the activities/resources on first click and collapses to hide them on second click; chevron orientation updates accordingly

test_cases.test_case: Open an activity/resource by clicking its name
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Course has at least one expanded section containing activities/resources
steps: 1. Open the Course page and expand the section that contains an activity/resource
2. Click the clickable name of an activity/resource
test_cases.expected_result: The selected activity/resource opens (navigates to the correct activity page or downloads/displays the resource)

test_cases.test_case: "Collapse all" collapses all expanded sections
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Course contains multiple sections and at least two sections are expanded
steps: 1. Open the Course page with multiple expanded sections
2. Click the "Collapse all" link at the top right of the section list
test_cases.expected_result: All sections collapse and no section content (activities/resources) is visible; chevrons show collapsed state

test_cases.test_case: Student cannot enable Edit mode on course page
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
steps: 2. Attempt to locate and enable any Edit mode toggle or link
test_cases.expected_result: Edit mode option is not available to the student, or attempt to enable it is blocked with a permission error/message

test_cases.test_case: Clicking a broken activity link displays an error
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Course contains an activity/resource with a broken URL or missing target
steps: 1. Open the Course page and expand the section containing the broken activity
2. Click the activity/resource name that points to the broken link
test_cases.expected_result: An error page or user-friendly error message is displayed (e.g., 404 or 'Resource not found') and the system does not crash

test_cases.test_case: Display of extremely long section names
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Course contains a section with an extremely long name (e.g., 500+ characters)
steps: 1. Open the Course page that includes the long-named section
2. Observe the layout of the section list and, if available, hover over the truncated name
test_cases.expected_result: Long section name does not break the layout; name is truncated with ellipsis if necessary and full name is available via tooltip or accessible method (no overflow causing UI distortion)

test_cases.test_case: Course page with a very large number of sections (performance and Collapse all)
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Course contains a very large number of sections (e.g., 200+)
steps: 1. Open the Course page with many sections
2. Observe page load time and responsiveness
test_cases.expected_result: Page loads within acceptable time (no hang); UI remains responsive and "Collapse all" collapses all sections without UI errors

test_cases.test_case: Course page when there are no sections
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Course has zero sections configured
steps: 1. Open the Course page for the empty course
test_cases.expected_result: Page shows an appropriate empty state (e.g., message like 'No sections available' or simply no section list); "Collapse all" link is not shown or is disabled and no errors appear

test_cases.test_case: Expand a section that contains no activities/resources
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Course has at least one section with zero activities/resources
steps: 2. Click the chevron to expand the section that contains no activities
test_cases.expected_result: Expanded section displays an appropriate empty state (e.g., 'No activities in this section' or no items), and no errors occur

test_cases.test_case: Activity/resource type icons are displayed and correspond to item type
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Course contains activities/resources of different types (e.g., PDF, Quiz, Forum)
steps: 1. Open the Course page and expand sections to view activities/resources
2. Verify the icon next to each activity/resource corresponds to its type
test_cases.expected_result: Each activity/resource displays an appropriate type icon next to its name (icons match the expected types) and icons are visible

test_cases.test_case: Keyboard accessibility for collapsing/expanding sections (Enter/Space)
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Course page is open with focusable chevron controls
steps: 1. Focus the chevron control for a section using keyboard (Tab key)
2. Press Enter (or Space) to toggle the section
test_cases.expected_result: Section toggles (expands/collapses) when the chevron is activated via keyboard; focus outline is visible and no JS errors occur

test_cases.test_case: "Collapse all" when all sections are already collapsed
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
All course sections are in collapsed state
steps: 1. Open the Course page ensuring all sections are collapsed
test_cases.expected_result: No visual change occurs, no errors are shown, and the page remains stable ("Collapse all" can be a no-op or remain available)

test_cases.test_case: Participants page shows list and expected columns
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
User has access to the course with enrolled participants
steps: 1. Navigate to the course Participants page
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table is displayed with columns: selection checkbox, First/Last name (sortable and linked), Roles, Groups, Last access to course, and filter controls are visible at the top

test_cases.test_case: Click a participant name opens their profile
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
At least one participant exists with a profile
steps: 2. Click on a participant's First/Last name link in the table
test_cases.expected_result: Participant's profile page/modal opens showing participant details; Student can view but does not see enrol/unenrol or role-edit controls

test_cases.test_case: Alphabetical filter by First name (A) filters results
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Participants list contains names starting with 'A' and other letters
steps: 2. In the First name alphabetical filter, click the 'A' button
test_cases.expected_result: Table shows only participants whose first name begins with 'A'; other rows are hidden

test_cases.test_case: Alphabetical filter by Last name (Z) filters results
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Participants list contains last names beginning with 'Z' and others
steps: 2. In the Last name alphabetical filter, click the 'Z' button
test_cases.expected_result: Table shows only participants whose last name begins with 'Z'; other rows are hidden

test_cases.test_case: Sort participants by First/Last name ascending and descending
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Participants list has multiple distinct first and last names
steps: 2. Click the First/Last name column header once to sort ascending
3. Click the same header again to sort descending
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking toggles the sort order; rows reorder accordingly (ascending then descending) and visible ordering matches expected sort

test_cases.test_case: Apply a single filter condition (Role = Student)
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Some participants have Role = Student
steps: 2. In the filter area, select the attribute 'Role' from the Select attribute dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Table displays only participants with Role = Student; filter indicator shows active filter

test_cases.test_case: Use '+ Add condition' with 'Any' toggle to create OR filter across attributes
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Participants exist matching either Role = Teacher or Group = Group1
steps: 2. Ensure the 'Any' toggle is enabled (OR behavior)
4. Click '+ Add condition' and select attribute 'Group' = 'Group1'
test_cases.expected_result: Table shows participants who satisfy either Role = Teacher OR Group = Group1 (union of matches); both conditions are visible in the filter UI

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to apply filters without selecting attribute/value
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Open Participants page
steps: 2. Click '+ Add condition' but do not select an attribute or value
test_cases.expected_result: Filter validation prevents application: an inline validation message is displayed (e.g., 'Please select an attribute and value') or no filter is applied and user is prompted to complete the condition

test_cases.test_case: Add conditions repeatedly until UI limit (max conditions) is reached
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Open Participants page
steps: 2. Repeatedly click '+ Add condition' and choose an attribute/value for each until the link is disabled or the system prevents adding more
3. Observe system behavior when the presumed limit is reached
test_cases.expected_result: System either allows a reasonable number of conditions and then disables 'Add condition' or shows a clear message about condition limit; UI remains stable and responsive

test_cases.test_case: Filter using special characters and unicode in values (e.g., O'Connor, 漢字)
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Participants include names/groups/attributes with special characters or unicode
steps: 2. In the filter area select attribute 'Last name' and enter a value containing special characters/unicode (e.g., "O'Connor" or "漢字")
test_cases.expected_result: Filter correctly returns participants with matching special-character/unicode values (no errors or mis-encoding); matches appear in the table

test_cases.test_case: Student cannot enrol or unenrol users (no enrollment controls present)
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Open Participants page
steps: 2. Inspect participant rows, profile pages, and any context menus for enrol/unenrol controls
3. If checkboxes are present, attempt any bulk action that would enrol/unenrol
test_cases.expected_result: No enrol/unenrol buttons or links are visible to the Student; bulk enrol/unenrol actions are not available or are disabled and produce no access to enrollment management

test_cases.test_case: Student cannot edit roles or access role-editing features
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Open Participants page
steps: 2. Inspect Roles column and any actions/context menus for edit role controls
3. Attempt to access any role-edit UI if visible
test_cases.expected_result: Role edit controls are not visible or are disabled for Student; any attempt to access role management is blocked or shows a permission error

test_cases.test_case: Clear filters returns to full participants list and resets UI
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Filters currently applied showing a subset of participants
steps: 1. On the Participants page, ensure a filter is active
test_cases.expected_result: All active filters are cleared, filter UI fields reset to defaults (Any toggle, Select attribute empty), and the table displays the full list of participants

test_cases.test_case: Empty participants list displays appropriate empty state
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Course has zero enrolled participants
test_cases.expected_result: Participants table shows an empty state message (e.g., 'No participants found') and filter controls remain available and usable; page does not error

test_cases.test_case: Very long participant names render safely (truncated/displayed) and profile link works
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
At least one participant has an extremely long first/last name (e.g., 256+ characters)
steps: 2. Locate the row with the very long name
3. Verify visual rendering and click the name link
test_cases.expected_result: Long names are visually handled (truncated with ellipsis or wrapped without breaking layout), UI remains usable, and clicking the name opens the participant profile

test_cases.test_case: Select multiple participant checkboxes but no bulk enrollment actions available to Student
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Participants table shows selectable checkboxes
steps: 3. Look for bulk action toolbar or buttons (e.g., Enrol/Unenrol/Edit roles)
test_cases.expected_result: Checkboxes can be selected/unselected, but no bulk enrol/unenrol or role-edit actions are available to the Student; if any bulk controls exist they are disabled or hidden

test_cases.test_case: Applying filters that produce zero matches shows empty results message
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
Filter inputs exist that will produce no matches
steps: 2. Enter filter criteria guaranteed to match no participants (e.g., Role = NonexistentRole)
test_cases.expected_result: Table shows no rows and a clear 'No results' or similar message; filters remain visible for adjustment

test_cases.test_case: Sorting with duplicate names maintains stable behavior and does not crash
preconditions: User is logged in as a Student
There are multiple participants with identical first and/or last names
steps: 2. Click the name column header to sort ascending or descending
3. Observe ordering of duplicate-name rows and attempt additional sorts
test_cases.expected_result: Sorting completes without error; rows with identical names are grouped consistently and UI remains responsive (secondary ordering may be by last access or other stable criterion)

test_cases.test_case: Grades page shows correct columns and AGGREGATION row
preconditions: Student is logged in
Student is enrolled in the course
At least one graded activity exists in the course
steps: 1. Navigate to the course Grades page (User report) from the course menu
2. Observe the grade table and bottom rows
test_cases.expected_result: Grade table displays columns: Grade item, Calculated weight, Grade, Range, Percentage, Feedback, Contribution to course total. An 'AGGREGATION Course total' row is present at the bottom showing the cumulative grade.

test_cases.test_case: Graded activity displays numeric grade, range and percentage correctly
preconditions: Student is logged in and enrolled
One graded activity exists with earned value 18 and range max 20
steps: 1. Open the Grades page for the course
test_cases.expected_result: Grade column shows '18' (or configured format), Range shows '0–20' (or '20'), Percentage shows '90%' (18/20), and Contribution to course total shows the calculated contribution based on weight.

test_cases.test_case: Course header collapses and expands to show/hide graded activities
preconditions: Student is logged in and enrolled
Course header contains multiple graded activities
steps: 2. Click the course name collapsible header
3. Click the header again to collapse
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking header expands to show indented graded activities; clicking again collapses them. The state persists while on the page.

test_cases.test_case: Contribution to course total reflects calculated weight
preconditions: Student is logged in and enrolled
Graded activity has a configured weight (e.g., 30%) and grade is available
steps: 2. Identify the activity with configured weight
3. Verify Contribution to course total value for that activity
test_cases.expected_result: Contribution equals (earned grade / range) * weight and is displayed in the Contribution to course total column. Values match expected calculation.

test_cases.test_case: Ungraded activity shows dash '–' in Grade column
preconditions: Student is logged in and enrolled
At least one activity exists with no grade yet
steps: 2. Locate the activity that has not been graded
test_cases.expected_result: Grade column displays '–' (dash) for ungraded activity, Percentage and Contribution show appropriate placeholders or zero values per spec, and no error is thrown.

test_cases.test_case: Student cannot view another student's grades via direct URL manipulation
preconditions: Student A is logged in and enrolled in course
Student B exists and has grades in the same course
Tester knows the URL or parameters that reference Student B's grade report
steps: 1. While logged in as Student A, attempt to open the grade report URL referencing Student B (e.g., change user id parameter)
test_cases.expected_result: System denies access (403/Access denied/redirect to own grades). Student A only sees their own grades; no other student's grades are shown.

test_cases.test_case: Student cannot access full gradebook or list of students
preconditions: Student is logged in
Course has a gradebook accessible to instructors/TAs
steps: 1. Navigate to any link/button that would normally access the full gradebook (if visible)
2. Attempt to open gradebook via direct URL if link not shown
test_cases.expected_result: Student cannot access the full gradebook or list of students. The UI either does not show links, or the system returns an access denied message/redirects to student's own grades.

test_cases.test_case: Zero-weight activity shows zero contribution and does not affect course total
preconditions: Student is logged in and enrolled
An activity exists with weight = 0 and has a grade
steps: 2. Locate the zero-weight activity and note Contribution and Course total
test_cases.expected_result: Contribution to course total for the zero-weight activity shows '0' (or 0.00). The AGGREGATION Course total excludes the zero-weight item and remains unchanged by this item's grade.

test_cases.test_case: Very long feedback text displayed without layout break
preconditions: Student is logged in
Instructor has entered very long feedback (e.g., 5000 characters) for an activity
steps: 2. Locate the activity with long feedback and view the Feedback column
3. Attempt to scroll or expand the feedback if UI offers truncation/tooltip
test_cases.expected_result: Long feedback is displayed or truncated with a visible expansion/tooltip. The page layout remains intact (no overflow breaking other columns) and text is readable or accessible via expansion.

test_cases.test_case: Page handles very large number of graded items (performance and visibility)
preconditions: Student is logged in
Course contains many graded items (e.g., 200+)
steps: 2. Scroll through the list to the bottom
3. Verify AGGREGATION Course total remains visible or reachable
test_cases.expected_result: Page loads within acceptable time, scrollbar or pagination appears if necessary, and AGGREGATION Course total is present and correct. No UI rendering errors occur.

test_cases.test_case: Percentage calculation is correct for fractional grades
preconditions: Student is logged in
An activity exists with earned value 7.5 and maximum 10
steps: 2. Locate the activity and check the Percentage column
test_cases.expected_result: Percentage displays '75%' (or '75.0%' depending on formatting). Calculation equals (7.5 / 10) * 100 and matches displayed value.

test_cases.test_case: Boundary grades 0 and maximum produce 0% and 100%
preconditions: Student is logged in
Two activities exist: one with grade 0/20, one with 20/20
steps: 2. Verify Percentage values for both boundary activities
test_cases.expected_result: Activity with 0/20 shows '0%'; activity with 20/20 shows '100%'. Contributions to course total reflect these percentages and weights.

test_cases.test_case: Grade fields are read-only for students (attempt to edit fails)
preconditions: Student is logged in and viewing Grades page
steps: 1. Attempt to click into Grade, Range, Percentage or Contribution fields to edit
2. Attempt to use browser dev tools to change displayed grade value and submit (if UI offers any submit)
3. Attempt to find edit controls for grades on the page
test_cases.expected_result: No edit controls are available. Fields are read-only. Any client-side changes do not persist; the server rejects unauthorized grade changes. Student cannot modify grades.

test_cases.test_case: Missing range value handled gracefully (no crash)
preconditions: Student is logged in
An activity exists with a missing or null maximum range value
steps: 2. Locate the activity with missing range
test_cases.expected_result: UI displays a placeholder for Range (e.g., '—' or 'N/A'), Percentage is shown as '—' or appropriate message. The page does not crash and other rows render normally.

test_cases.test_case: Feedback column displays instructor feedback for graded item
preconditions: Student is logged in
Instructor has provided feedback for at least one graded activity
steps: 2. Locate the activity with feedback and view the Feedback column
test_cases.expected_result: Feedback column shows the instructor's feedback text. If feedback is long it follows UI behavior (truncated with expansion or fully visible).

test_cases.test_case: Contributions and AGGREGATION total rounding consistent and sums to expected total
preconditions: Student is logged in
Multiple graded activities with weights lead to fractional contributions requiring rounding
steps: 2. Note individual Contribution to course total values and the AGGREGATION Course total
3. Sum individual displayed contributions and compare to AGGREGATION value
test_cases.expected_result: Displayed individual contributions are rounded per UI spec (e.g., two decimals) and the AGGREGATION Course total equals the sum of contributions within acceptable rounding tolerance. No significant discrepancy is present.

test_cases.test_case: Assignment page displays Opened date, Due date, and full Description
preconditions: User is a student enrolled in the course
An assignment exists with Opened date, Due date, and Description populated
test_cases.expected_result: Opened date, Due date, and the full Description are visible and match the assignment data

test_cases.test_case: Add submission button opens submission form with online text editor
preconditions: Assignment is configured with an online text editor
Current time is between Opened date and Due date (submissions allowed)
test_cases.expected_result: Submission form opens and contains an online text editor input area

test_cases.test_case: Add submission form displays file upload area when configured
preconditions: Assignment is configured to allow file uploads
Student is on the Assignment page
test_cases.expected_result: Submission form includes a file upload area with controls to select files

test_cases.test_case: Successful submission using online text editor
preconditions: Assignment accepts online text submissions
Submission period is open
steps: 2. Enter valid text into the online editor
3. Click 'Save changes' or 'Submit' (as provided by UI)
test_cases.expected_result: Submission is saved; Submission status shows 'Submitted for grading' (or appropriate), Last modified shows current timestamp, and submission content is viewable

test_cases.test_case: Successful file upload submission
preconditions: Assignment accepts file uploads
Student has a valid file within allowed size and type
Submission period is open
steps: 2. Use the file upload area to attach the valid file
test_cases.expected_result: File is uploaded and listed in the submission preview; Submission status updates to 'Submitted for grading' and Last modified shows current timestamp

test_cases.test_case: Successful submission with both text and file when assignment allows both
preconditions: Assignment configured to allow both online text and file upload
Submission window is open
steps: 2. Enter text into the text editor and attach a valid file
test_cases.expected_result: Both text and file are saved in the submission; Submission status shows submitted and Last modified updated

test_cases.test_case: Student can view and edit their submission before due date when resubmission permitted
preconditions: Student has already submitted
Current time is before Due date
Teacher allows resubmission
steps: 3. Modify submission (edit text or replace file) and save
test_cases.expected_result: Edits are saved; Last modified updates; Submission status remains or updates accordingly

test_cases.test_case: Graded submission displays earned grade and written feedback
preconditions: Student submitted an assignment
Teacher has graded the submission and provided written feedback
steps: 1. Student opens the Assignment page after grading is completed
test_cases.expected_result: Earned grade and teacher's written feedback are displayed on the page in the grading section

test_cases.test_case: Submitting empty required online text shows validation error
preconditions: Assignment requires online text or non-empty submission
Submission window is open
steps: 2. Leave the online text editor empty
test_cases.expected_result: System prevents submission and shows an error indicating the submission cannot be empty or that required content is missing

test_cases.test_case: Uploading disallowed file type is rejected with error
preconditions: Assignment allows file uploads but restricts file types (e.g., only .pdf, .docx)
Student has a disallowed file type (e.g., .exe)
steps: 2. Attempt to attach a disallowed file type
test_cases.expected_result: Upload is rejected; an error message explains the disallowed file type and submission is not accepted

test_cases.test_case: Uploading file exceeding size limit shows error and prevents submission
preconditions: Assignment has a maximum allowed file size (e.g., 20 MB)
Student attempts to upload a file larger than limit
test_cases.expected_result: System rejects the file and shows an error indicating the file exceeds the allowed size; submission not saved

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to submit after due date when resubmission is not permitted is rejected
preconditions: Current time is after Due date
Teacher does not permit submissions past due date (no late submissions/resubmissions)
Student has not yet submitted or tries to resubmit
steps: 1. Open Assignment page after due date
2. Click 'Add submission' (if present) or attempt to submit
test_cases.expected_result: System prevents new submissions or resubmissions and shows an appropriate message indicating submission window has closed

test_cases.test_case: Submit a file at the exact maximum allowed size
preconditions: Assignment has a known file size limit (e.g., 20 MB)
Student has a file exactly at the limit
steps: 2. Attach the file that is exactly the maximum size
test_cases.expected_result: File is accepted and submission succeeds; submission listed and Last modified updated

test_cases.test_case: Submit extremely long text (boundary length) in online editor
preconditions: Assignment accepts online text
There is a maximum character limit enforced (or not stated) — test uses very large but valid length
steps: 2. Paste or type a very large body of text at the expected maximum or slightly below (boundary)
test_cases.expected_result: If within allowed limits, submission is accepted and text is stored/displayed; if limits exist, an informative validation error is shown that explains the limit

test_cases.test_case: Filename with special/unicode characters is accepted and displayed correctly
preconditions: Assignment accepts file uploads
Student has a file with special characters/unicode in filename
test_cases.expected_result: File uploads successfully and filename is displayed correctly in the submission preview and summary

test_cases.test_case: Time remaining shows 0 or negative when due date equals current time or has just passed
preconditions: Current time is exactly at or just after the Due date
Assignment page is open
steps: 1. Refresh or open the Assignment page at the boundary time
2. Observe the Time remaining field in the Submission status section
test_cases.expected_result: Time remaining displays '0' or an appropriate 'time has passed' message; UI does not crash and displays clear status (e.g., 'Due date passed')

test_cases.test_case: Submission comments field rejects excessively long comment input if a limit exists
preconditions: Submission comments field is present
A comment length limit is configured
steps: 2. Enter a comment that exceeds the configured limit
test_cases.expected_result: System prevents submission of the overly long comment and displays an error indicating the comment exceeds the allowed length

test_cases.test_case: Submission status summary shows correct Grading status before and after grading
preconditions: Student has submitted the assignment
Teacher has or has not graded the submission (test both states)
steps: 1. After student submission, open Assignment page and note Grading status
2. After teacher grades, reopen the page
test_cases.expected_result: Before grading, Grading status shows 'Not graded' (or similar); after grading, Grading status shows 'Graded' and grade/feedback appear

test_cases.test_case: No Due date configured (open-ended assignment) displays appropriate Time remaining or message
preconditions: Assignment has no Due date (open-ended)
Student opens the Assignment page
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page for an assignment without a Due date
2. Observe the Time remaining and Submission status section
test_cases.expected_result: The page displays an appropriate message (e.g., 'No due date' or 'Open until closed by teacher') and Time remaining either hidden or shows 'N/A' without errors

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to edit submission after due date when resubmission not allowed shows blocked action
preconditions: Student has a prior submission
Current time is after Due date
Teacher has not allowed resubmission after due date
steps: 1. Open the Assignment page after the due date
2. Click 'View/edit submission' or attempt to modify submission
test_cases.expected_result: Editing is blocked or disabled; system displays a message that resubmission is not permitted after the due date

test_cases.test_case: Assignments section expanded by default and table columns visible
preconditions: User is logged in
Course contains at least one assignment
steps: 1. Navigate to the course Activities page
test_cases.expected_result: The Assignments section is expanded by default and displays a table with columns: Name (clickable link), Due date, and Submission status.

test_cases.test_case: Clicking an activity name navigates to that activity's page
preconditions: User is logged in
At least one assignment exists in the course
steps: 2. Click on an assignment Name link in the Assignments table
test_cases.expected_result: Browser navigates to the selected activity's page (URL changes and activity page content loads).

test_cases.test_case: Activity Name shows parent section displayed below it in the table
preconditions: User is logged in
An assignment exists and is part of a parent section
steps: 2. Inspect the Name cell for the assignment
test_cases.expected_result: The Name cell contains a clickable link for the activity and the parent section name is displayed below the activity name (visible in the same row/cell).

test_cases.test_case: Forums and Resources sections are collapsed by default
preconditions: User is logged in
Course contains forums and resources
test_cases.expected_result: Forum and Resources sections appear collapsed by default and display an expand/collapse arrow indicator.

test_cases.test_case: Expanding a collapsed section (Forums) displays its activities
preconditions: User is logged in
Course contains at least one forum activity
steps: 2. Click the expand arrow on the Forums section
test_cases.expected_result: The Forums section expands and lists the forum activities (each activity name shown, clickable).

test_cases.test_case: Clicking an activity name in a non-Assignments section navigates to that activity
preconditions: User is logged in
At least one activity exists in Forums or Resources
steps: 3. Click an activity name in that section
test_cases.expected_result: The application navigates to the selected activity's page and the activity page content loads.

test_cases.test_case: Additional activity types appear as their own collapsible sections
preconditions: User is logged in
Course contains at least one additional activity type (e.g., Quizzes, Workshops)
test_cases.expected_result: Each additional activity type appears as its own section on the page and is collapsed by default with an expand arrow.

test_cases.test_case: Assignments table shows correct submission statuses
preconditions: User is logged in
Course contains assignments with different submission statuses (e.g., Submitted, Not submitted, Late)
steps: 2. Inspect the Submission status column in the Assignments table
test_cases.expected_result: Each assignment row displays the correct submission status corresponding to the backend data.

test_cases.test_case: User without permission attempts to open an activity -> access denied
preconditions: User is logged in with insufficient permissions to view a specific activity
That activity is listed on the Activities page
steps: 2. Click the activity Name the user should not have access to
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents access and displays an access denied message or redirects to an appropriate error/permission page.

test_cases.test_case: Clicking a listed activity whose backend record was deleted results in 404 or not-found error
preconditions: User is logged in
An activity is listed on the Activities page but its backend record has been deleted
steps: 2. Click the deleted activity's Name link
test_cases.expected_result: The application shows a 404 / "Activity not found" page or an appropriate error message indicating the activity no longer exists.

test_cases.test_case: Navigating directly to an invalid activity id in the URL shows an error
preconditions: User is logged in
steps: 1. Enter an activity URL with an invalid/non-numeric or nonexistent activity id (e.g., /mod/activity/view.php?id=INVALID) in the browser address bar
test_cases.expected_result: The system displays an error message (e.g., "Activity not found" or 404) and does not display an activity page.

test_cases.test_case: Network/server failure when loading Activities page shows error banner
preconditions: User is logged in
Simulate network outage or server returns 5xx response for activities list
steps: 1. Attempt to open the Activities page while the network/server is failing
test_cases.expected_result: A visible error banner or message appears (e.g., "Failed to load activities"), and the UI provides a retry option or guidance.

test_cases.test_case: Course with no activities shows appropriate empty states
preconditions: User is logged in
Course contains zero activities of any type
test_cases.expected_result: The page shows empty state messaging where appropriate (e.g., Assignments table shows "No activities" or similar) and no activity rows are listed.

test_cases.test_case: Extremely long activity name is displayed truncated with full text available
preconditions: User is logged in
An activity exists with a very long name (> 255 characters)
steps: 2. Locate the long activity name in the table
test_cases.expected_result: The name is truncated in the table (ellipses) to maintain layout; the full name is available via tooltip/hover or by clicking into the activity page.

test_cases.test_case: Activity names with special characters and Unicode render correctly
preconditions: User is logged in
An activity exists with special characters and Unicode (e.g., emojis, non-Latin scripts)
test_cases.expected_result: The activity name displays correctly without garbled characters or layout breaks (Unicode and special characters render properly).

test_cases.test_case: Large number of activities loads and is scrollable (performance)
preconditions: User is logged in
Course contains a very large number of activities (e.g., hundreds or thousands)
steps: 2. Measure load time and scroll through the list/sections
test_cases.expected_result: The Activities page loads within acceptable performance limits, sections remain responsive, and users can scroll through activities (UI may use pagination or lazy loading).

test_cases.test_case: Assignments with missing due date display 'No due date' or appropriate placeholder
preconditions: User is logged in
At least one assignment exists with no due date set (null)
steps: 2. Locate the assignment row with no due date
test_cases.expected_result: The Due date column for that assignment shows a clear placeholder such as "No due date" (not an error or invalid date).

test_cases.test_case: Clicking an activity name opens the activity in the same tab
preconditions: User is logged in
At least one activity exists
test_cases.expected_result: The activity opens in the same browser tab (the current tab navigates to the activity page rather than opening a new tab), consistent with expected navigation behavior.

test_cases.test_case: Profile page displays core elements
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
User navigates to own Profile page
steps: 2. Observe the page header and main area
test_cases.expected_result: Student's circular initials icon, full name, Message button, optional profile description (if any), and information cards (User details, Privacy and policies, Course details, Miscellaneous, Reports, Login activity) are visible and correctly labeled

test_cases.test_case: Message button opens messaging interface
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
On their own Profile page or another student's profile that allows messaging
steps: 1. Click the Message button on the Profile page
test_cases.expected_result: System opens the messaging interface (modal or new page) prefilled with the recipient; user can compose a message

test_cases.test_case: Edit profile link opens profile form with collapsible panels
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Viewing own Profile page
steps: 1. Click the 'Edit profile' link in the User details card
test_cases.expected_result: Profile form opens with collapsible panels: General, User picture, Additional names, Interests, Optional fields; form fields are visible within each expanded panel

test_cases.test_case: Update profile with valid inputs saves changes
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
steps: 2. Modify First name, Last name, and Description with valid text
3. Choose a Country and Timezone from dropdowns
test_cases.expected_result: Form validates inputs, saves changes, returns to Profile page showing updated name and description

test_cases.test_case: Course details links open enrolled course profiles
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
User is enrolled in at least one course
On Profile page
steps: 1. In the Course details card, click a linked course profile
test_cases.expected_result: Clicked course profile opens in the same tab or a new tab (depending on implementation) and displays the course's profile page

test_cases.test_case: Login activity shows exact dates and relative times
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile page has both first and last access recorded
steps: 1. Open the Profile page and locate Login activity card
test_cases.expected_result: First and Last access show exact date/time (e.g., 2026-06-01 14:23) and a relative time indicator (e.g., '2 days ago') next to each

test_cases.test_case: Data retention summary link opens policy page
preconditions: User is logged in
On Profile page
steps: 1. In Privacy and policies card, click the Data retention summary link
test_cases.expected_result: Data retention summary page opens and displays relevant policy information

test_cases.test_case: Upload a valid profile picture succeeds
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
Valid image file available (supported format, within size limit)
steps: 2. Click to upload a new picture and select a valid image file
test_cases.expected_result: Image uploads successfully, thumbnail updates on Profile page to show the new picture

test_cases.test_case: Cancel in edit form discards changes
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
Some fields modified but not saved
steps: 1. Modify one or more fields in the edit form
test_cases.expected_result: Form closes and Profile page remains unchanged (no modified values saved)

test_cases.test_case: Cannot edit another student's profile
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Viewing another student's Profile page
steps: 2. Look for 'Edit profile' link or attempt to navigate to edit URL directly
test_cases.expected_result: 'Edit profile' link is not available or disabled; direct navigation to edit URL is blocked and an authorization error or access denied message is shown

test_cases.test_case: Missing required field (Email) causes validation error
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
test_cases.expected_result: Form prevents submission and shows an inline error message indicating that Email address is required

test_cases.test_case: Invalid email format is rejected
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
test_cases.expected_result: Form prevents submission and shows an inline error message indicating invalid email format

test_cases.test_case: Uploading unsupported image format is rejected
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
File of unsupported format (e.g., .exe) available
steps: 2. Attempt to upload an unsupported file format
test_cases.expected_result: Upload is rejected with an error message indicating unsupported file type; picture is not changed

test_cases.test_case: Uploading oversized image is rejected
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
Image file larger than allowed limit available
steps: 2. Attempt to upload an image exceeding the size limit
test_cases.expected_result: Upload is rejected with an error message indicating file size exceeds limit; picture is not changed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to set empty First name triggers validation error
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
test_cases.expected_result: Form prevents submission and displays an inline error indicating First name is required

test_cases.test_case: Very long names and description are accepted/truncated appropriately
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
Strings at or above expected maximum lengths prepared
steps: 2. Enter a 500-character First name and Last name each and a 2000-character Description
test_cases.expected_result: System either accepts and saves long values (displaying them appropriately) or enforces and displays a clear validation/truncation message; no crash occurs

test_cases.test_case: Special characters and non-Latin scripts in names
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
steps: 2. Enter names using special characters and non-Latin scripts (e.g., emojis, Cyrillic, Chinese)
test_cases.expected_result: System saves and displays names correctly (or provides a validation message if characters are disallowed); no layout breakage

test_cases.test_case: Timezone dropdown boundary values selectable
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
steps: 2. Select extreme timezone values (e.g., UTC-12 and UTC+14) from the Timezone dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Selected timezone is saved and reflected in Profile and any displayed timestamps; no error shown

test_cases.test_case: Set email visibility to 'Hide my email address' and confirm effect
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Another test account available to view profile
steps: 1. Open Profile edit form and set Email visibility dropdown to 'Hide my email address'
3. Log out and log in as another test user, then view original user's Profile page
test_cases.expected_result: Email address is not visible to other users per visibility setting; original user can still see their own email

test_cases.test_case: Upload image exactly at maximum allowed size
preconditions: User is logged in as a student
Profile edit form open
Image exactly at size limit available
steps: 2. Upload the image file that equals the maximum allowed size
test_cases.expected_result: Image is accepted and saved if system allows boundary size; no error thrown and thumbnail updates

test_cases.test_case: Basic logout via UI menu
preconditions: User is authenticated
User is on a protected page (e.g., dashboard)
steps: 1. Click the user/profile menu or hamburger menu where the 'Log out' control is located
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page (login form visible). Current session is terminated (session cookie removed or invalidated) and attempting to navigate to a protected page redirects to the login page requiring re-authentication.

test_cases.test_case: Logout via direct /logout endpoint
preconditions: User is authenticated
User can access application URL bar
steps: 1. Enter the application's logout endpoint URL (e.g., /logout) and navigate
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page and the authenticated session is terminated; protected pages require re-login.

test_cases.test_case: Logout from a deep/protected page (settings)
preconditions: User is authenticated
User is on a deep protected page (e.g., Settings > Account)
steps: 1. Click the 'Log out' control from the page header/footer/menu
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page and session is terminated; attempting to reload or navigate back to the deep page requires authentication.

test_cases.test_case: Click logout when no active session (not authenticated)
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no valid session)
steps: 1. Navigate to the application or click a visible 'Log out' link/button (if present)
test_cases.expected_result: Application redirects to the login page or shows the login screen without error; user remains unauthenticated and no server error is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Network failure during logout action
preconditions: User is authenticated
Test environment allows simulating network failure (e.g., offline mode or blocked request)
steps: 1. Simulate network failure or block the logout request
test_cases.expected_result: Application displays an error or retry prompt indicating logout failed (or informs the user of connectivity issue). The client may remain on the current page and should not assume the session was terminated until confirmation.

test_cases.test_case: Use browser back button after successful logout
preconditions: User is authenticated and on a protected page
steps: 1. Click 'Log out' and confirm redirection to login page
3. Observe whether the protected page is accessible
test_cases.expected_result: Protected page is not accessible; the app redirects to the login page or shows the login form. Content from the protected page must not be available without re-authentication.

test_cases.test_case: Logout in one tab invalidates session in another tab
preconditions: User is authenticated in two separate browser tabs/windows using the same account
steps: 1. In Tab A, click 'Log out' and confirm redirected to login page
2. In Tab B, refresh a protected page or try to perform an authenticated action
3. Observe authentication state in Tab B
test_cases.expected_result: Tab B is required to re-authenticate: refresh leads to login page or shows authentication error; protected actions are blocked until login.

test_cases.test_case: Double-click or rapidly click logout control
preconditions: User is authenticated
Logout control is visible and clickable
steps: 1. Rapidly click or double-click the 'Log out' control
2. Observe application behavior and any messages
test_cases.expected_result: Logout is processed once without causing duplicate actions or errors; user ends up on the login page and session is terminated cleanly.

test_cases.test_case: Logout request rejected due to missing/invalid CSRF token
preconditions: User is authenticated
Test environment allows tampering with outgoing requests (e.g., proxy)
steps: 1. Intercept and remove or modify the CSRF token/header from the logout request
test_cases.expected_result: Server rejects the logout request (e.g., 403) and client indicates the action failed; the user remains authenticated until a valid logout request succeeds.
